concerns.text: Tasks implement 'Virtual Cohort Matching' (individual-level) and 'Distributional Comparison' (Path A/B) which fundamentally contradicts the Spec's mandated 'Ecological Correlation' (stratum-level aggregation). The Spec (US-1, US-2) requires aggregating into demographic strata and correlating means, whereas tasks T013-T015 attempt to match individual subjects and T022a/T022b switch logic based on match counts. This is a semantic weakening of the core research requirement.

Task T013 mandates downloading OpenNeuro dataset 'ds000246'. The Spec (US-1, FR-002) and Constitution (Principle VII) explicitly mandate 'ds000248'. Using a different dataset violates the Single Source of Truth and specific dataset constraints.

Task T015 implements a fallback to Path B with 'exit code 0' if <10 pairs are found. The Spec (US-1, Edge Cases) explicitly mandates exiting with 'code 1' if valid strata are <5. The task silently relaxes the error-handling constraint and changes the success criteria logic.

Task T022a implements correlation on 'top PCoA axes'. The Spec (FR-006, US-2) explicitly mandates correlation on 'the 20 taxa with the highest mean relative abundance'. Replacing the specific taxon list with PCoA axes is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement.

Tasks T014 and T014a propose two different matching algorithms (Nearest-Neighbor vs. Propensity Score) for the same output without a decision criterion. This introduces ambiguity in the implementation of the matching logic required by the plan, potentially leading to non-reproducible results if the choice is arbitrary.
